title: Officer quick reference guide to occupations - Canada.ca
content:
## Officer quick reference guide to occupations
This section contains policy, procedures and guidance used by IRCC staff. It is posted on the department’s website as a courtesy to stakeholders.
Use the below table to quickly reference details on occupations.
Accident or incident inspectors (aviation)
Aviation accident or incident inspectors
Under authority of Canadian Transportation Accident Investigation and Safety Board Act
Agri-Food Pilot (AFP) Program
Non-seasonal work such as mushroom and greenhouse crop production, meat processing and livestock raising industries
Bridging open work permit (BOWP)
The AFP Program is an economic pilot program designed to attract experienced, non-seasonal workers who can settle in Canada. BOWP applications must be submitted online.
The AFP Program is an economic pilot program designed to attract experienced, non-seasonal workers who can settle in Canada.
BOWP applications must be submitted online.
Foreign-owned and registered aircraft or under wet lease arrangement
Canadian aircraft or dry lease arrangement
Operational, ground and technical personnel
If they qualify as intra-company transferee
Airline Telecommunication and Information Services (SITA)
Arts and entertainment occupations
Actors, directors, performing artists, technicians, creative personnel
Required (unless exempt under Essential to TV/Film Production [C14] or Performing Arts [C23])
Canadian film, TV, theatre, and Internet or radio broadcasts
